query: We want our product team to spin up new deposit and loan products without waiting on an engineering release — can AccountForge do that?
gold_answer_summary: Yes — product creation is configuration-driven in AccountForge, the product factory in Meridian Ledger Core. Business users assemble products from reusable blocks (interest rules, fees, eligibility, statements), test in a sandbox, and promote via approval workflow, cutting time-to-market without code changes.

query: Can the core keep posting and showing live balances 24/7 including weekends, instead of going down for a nightly batch?
gold_answer_summary: Yes — Meridian Ledger Core uses continuous processing rather than an overnight batch, posting in real time with immediately available balances across channels. End-of-day, month-end, and accrual activities run as non-blocking background tasks against a consistent snapshot, so customers keep transacting while they complete.

query: When does the system actually capitalise accrued interest onto the balance, and where are the accrual rate and frequency set up?
gold_answer_summary: Interest is parameterised per product in AccountForge (rate basis, day-count, accrual frequency, capitalisation schedule). It accrues daily on the cleared balance and is moved to principal at the configured capitalisation event — monthly, quarterly, or at maturity — generating the matching GL and tax entries.

query: What's the difference between blocking an account and just earmarking funds, and how does each affect available balance?
gold_answer_summary: A full block stops debits/credits (legal or fraud holds); an earmark/amount hold reserves a sum, cutting available balance while leaving the ledger balance unchanged (card auths, cheque holds). Each carries a reason code, originator, and expiry; available balance subtracts active earmarks in real time per currency, and all placements/releases are audited.

query: If a caller retries the same posting request, how does the Nexus Posting Engine avoid applying it twice?
gold_answer_summary: Each instruction carries a client idempotency key recorded in a durable dedupe store within the same transaction as the ledger movement; a repeat key returns the original result. Movements use balanced, atomic double-entry batches with per-account version sequencing, and committed movements emit immutable events that idempotent consumers can safely replay — giving exactly-once financial effect under retries.

query: How does Meridian keep the customer sub-ledger continuously reconciled with the GL instead of discovering breaks at month-end?
gold_answer_summary: Customer movements generate GL postings via accounting templates mapping product/transaction/currency to debit/credit accounts (structured by entity, branch, currency, class). A continuous reconciliation sums sub-ledger balances by bucket against GL control accounts and raises an exception with the contributing entries on any divergence; suspense/clearing items are auto-aged, so every GL balance drills back to source transactions.

query: If we post a transaction with a value date in the past, does the system recompute interest and fees for the affected period?
gold_answer_summary: Yes — the Nexus Posting Engine recomputes value-date-sensitive effects: accruals are recalculated from the value date forward with adjustment entries, balance-dependent fees re-evaluated, and the available/overdraft position reconstructed as of the value date. All adjustments are new dated entries (history is never mutated), affected statements are flagged for regeneration, and how far back is allowed is limited by product/role with approval for large back-valuations.

query: Does the payments hub support instant and real-time payment rails natively, or is that a bolt-on?
gold_answer_summary: Native — PayBridge handles instant/real-time schemes as a core feature, processing around the clock with sub-second internal handling. It is scheme-agnostic: instant schemes, RTGS, and batch clearings run through a common core with scheme-specific adapters, so a new scheme is added by configuring an adapter rather than rebuilding the hub.

query: How does PayBridge keep payment operations lean and stop failed payments from disappearing silently?
gold_answer_summary: PayBridge maximises straight-through processing: payments are validated, enriched, and routed automatically, and any that can't proceed go to a focused exception queue with the reason and context attached. Operations work a single console across all schemes, repair rules auto-correct common formatting issues, and dashboards show STP rates and exception causes.

query: What does FlowRoute weigh when deciding which clearing channel a given payment should go through?
gold_answer_summary: FlowRoute selects the best clearing channel per payment by configurable policy, weighing beneficiary reachability, required speed, scheme cut-offs, and cost — routing via instant rails, RTGS, or cheaper batch schemes with fallbacks if the primary is unavailable. The bank defines policy in business terms, such as the cheapest channel meeting the chosen delivery time.

query: What restrictions can we place on instant payments — both the timing windows and the maximum amount a customer can send?
gold_answer_summary: Timing is governed by per-scheme cut-off and processing-window controls in FlowRoute, which parks and auto-releases payments around settlement windows with holiday/calendar handling (kb_021). Monetary caps come from a separate value-limit framework — per-transaction maximums by scheme/channel/segment plus rolling cumulative limits, tiered by verification and evaluated in real time across channels (kb_022).

query: When a beneficiary bank sends a payment back, or a customer asks to recall one, how does the hub handle it?
gold_answer_summary: PayBridge matches returns to the original instruction via preserved end-to-end/scheme references, credits funds back, and records the return reason. Customer recall requests generate the scheme recall message and track the response; reversals of erroneous/duplicate payments book offsetting entries rather than deleting history. Each flow respects scheme time limits and lands in dedicated operations queues.

query: For SEPA Instant, how does the hub turn an incoming pacs.008 into the pacs.002 status messages back to the originator, including timeouts?
gold_answer_summary: PayBridge ingests each pacs.008 and creates an internal payment keyed by the End-to-End Id plus its own reference; FlowRoute validates reachability before sending. It emits pacs.002 statuses — ACCP/ACSC on confirmation or RJCT with a structured reason code (e.g. AB05 timeout, AC04 closed account) — preserving the original identifiers. The SCT Inst ten-second SLA is enforced via a configurable timeout that auto-reverses and returns a RJCT.

query: If the same payment file gets submitted twice by mistake, how does the system stop the beneficiaries being paid again?
gold_answer_summary: FlowRoute records processed file identifiers so a resubmitted file is rejected, and fingerprints each instruction (debtor, creditor, amount, currency, date, reference) combined with any end-to-end id into an idempotency key; a repeat within the window is held as a probable duplicate for review. Key persistence is transactional with payment creation, so a retry after partial failure can't create a second payment. Genuine repeats are distinguished by date or a unique reference.

query: Does the platform handle loan origination — application intake, credit scoring, and underwriting decisions?
gold_answer_summary: No supporting entry — the KB covers product configuration, accruals, and posting in the core, but not origination workflows, credit scoring, or underwriting. Expected behaviour is to state no relevant content was found rather than fabricate a capability.

query: Is there a dedicated module for letters of credit, collections, and guarantees, or is trade finance handled off-platform?
gold_answer_summary: Yes — TradeWeave is a dedicated module covering documentary credits, documentary collections, guarantees/standby LCs, and supply-chain finance. It digitises the full instrument lifecycle (application, issuance, amendment, document examination, settlement) for both corporate clients and the bank's trade-operations team.

query: How does the bank keep trade finance instruments within a customer's approved credit limits?
gold_answer_summary: TradeWeave integrates with the limit/exposure framework: issuing an LC or guarantee records contingent exposure against customer and counterparty limits, and utilisation, expiry, or cancellation updates it in real time. Limit checks at issuance prevent breaches, and reporting aggregates trade exposure by customer, country, and product for concentration and regulatory management.

query: Walk me through the full lifecycle of an import letter of credit — both getting it issued and later changing its terms.
gold_answer_summary: Issuance (kb_033): the applicant submits LC terms, TradeWeave validates completeness, reserves the credit line for contingent exposure, routes for approval, then generates the issuance message and contingent-liability accounting. Amendment (kb_034): changes to a live LC show before/after values, recalculate exposure and the reserved line, route for approval, track beneficiary consent where required, and generate the amendment message — with terms treated as changed only once effective.

query: When documents are presented under an LC, how does the bank check them for discrepancies and decide what to do?
gold_answer_summary: TradeWeave drives a structured examination against a checklist derived from the LC terms, recording each deviation as a formal discrepancy with a reference and tracking the examination clock. Where discrepancies exist it manages the options — seek the applicant's waiver, return the documents, or send on approval — records the decision and applicant response, and applies discrepancy fees.

query: Can the platform issue and manage demand guarantees and standby LCs through claims and expiry?
gold_answer_summary: Yes — TradeWeave handles demand guarantees and standby LCs across their lifecycle: issuance from templated or bespoke wordings, contingent exposure recorded against limits and released on expiry/cancellation, tracking of expiry and claim periods with reminders, amendments and extend-or-pay demands, and claim examination/approval with settlement entries. Counter-guarantee arrangements are supported.

query: How does the trade module produce MT700-type messages and read incoming ones without manual rekeying?
gold_answer_summary: TradeWeave keeps a structured representation of each instrument and maps it to message formats — generating MT700 (and MT707 amendments, MT710 advising) populated from captured terms so records and messages can't diverge. Incoming messages are parsed back with mandatory-field/format validation (rejects queued for repair). ISO 20022 trade messages are supported, with DocuTrade providing translation during the transition.

query: Can the bank accept and examine trade documents presented electronically under eUCP rules, using DocuTrade?
gold_answer_summary: Yes — DocuTrade extends TradeWeave to handle electronic presentation under electronic-record rules such as the eUCP supplement: the beneficiary presents records via a secure channel, DocuTrade verifies authenticity/integrity (flagging unverifiable or corrupt records as discrepancies), applies the same term-derived examination checklist, and manages electronic-presentation timing rules, archiving all records and outcomes.

query: Does Meridian include wealth management — investment portfolios, mutual funds, and robo-advisory for retail customers?
gold_answer_summary: No supporting entry — the KB covers core banking, payments, trade finance, treasury (the bank's own positions), digital channels, and compliance, but not customer investment/wealth products or advisory. Expected behaviour is to state no relevant content was found rather than fabricate one.

query: How does treasury get a real-time view of cash across accounts and currencies to manage intraday liquidity?
gold_answer_summary: TreasuryHub's LiquidView consolidates cash positions across accounts, currencies, and entities into one real-time view. Intraday it shows expected and actual flows (including large payments) to manage nostro balances; structurally it supports cash-flow forecasting and liquidity-metric monitoring, with alerts on projected shortfalls or threshold breaches, fed by real-time payment flows.

query: What stops a dealer from trading outside approved limits or booking deals at off-market rates?
gold_answer_summary: TreasuryHub checks limits (product, currency, counterparty, tenor, position size) as trades are captured, blocking or escalating breaches, with stop-loss/position limits for market risk and counterparty limits for credit risk. Segregation of duties separates dealing from confirmation/settlement, unusual activity like off-market rates or out-of-hours dealing is flagged, every deal is logged, and dashboards show limit utilisation in real time.

query: What does RateForge do to catch a bad or stale price tick before those rates feed valuations and pricing?
gold_answer_summary: RateForge ingests rates, curves, and benchmarks from external and internal sources, runs validation such as tolerance and staleness checks to catch bad ticks, and can blend or prioritise sources. It distributes one validated set used consistently for deal-rate checking, revaluation, and customer pricing, retains history for audit, and logs authorised overrides.

query: How are FX spot and forward deals captured, and then how are those positions revalued for mark-to-market?
gold_answer_summary: Capture (kb_046): TreasuryHub records currency pair, amount, rate, value date, and counterparty (forwards capturing forward points), checks counterparty/position limits and off-market rates, then updates the position in real time and generates confirmations and both-leg settlement instructions. Revaluation (kb_047): on a scheduled basis it marks net positions and outstanding forwards to current rates from RateForge, posting unrealised gains/losses to revaluation/P&L accounts, while realised results are recognised at maturity or close-out.

query: How does treasury handle interbank placements, term deposits, and short-term borrowings end to end?
gold_answer_summary: TreasuryHub captures principal, rate, value/maturity dates, day-count, and counterparty (limit-checked), accrues interest daily over each deal's life, settles interest and principal on agreed dates, and supports rollovers and early breaks with recalculation. Cash flows feed LiquidView's funding view; confirmations, settlement instructions, and accounting are generated automatically, with maturity-ladder reporting.

query: How are yield curves built and used consistently for discounting cash flows across treasury?
gold_answer_summary: RateForge builds per-currency curves from market instruments (deposits at the short end, swaps/bonds further out), validates the points, and bootstraps a zero-coupon curve with configurable interpolation. The discount factors present-value future cash flows when revaluing forwards, swaps, and money-market positions. Curves are versioned with a timestamp so any valuation is reproducible, and multiple curves per currency (discounting vs projection) are supported.

query: Can the bank custody and trade crypto or digital assets for customers on this platform?
gold_answer_summary: No supporting entry — the KB covers fiat core banking, payments, trade finance, treasury, channels, and compliance, with no mention of crypto or digital-asset custody/trading. Expected behaviour is to state no relevant content was found rather than invent a capability.

query: How does Meridian make sure mobile, web, and branch staff all see the same balances and features?
gold_answer_summary: Channels are built on a shared services layer, ChannelOne, that exposes the same capabilities and data to mobile, web, and assisted channels — so a customer can continue an action across channels, balances/limits/product info are identical everywhere, and branch/call-centre staff see what the customer sees. New features are built once and offered everywhere, and personalisation and entitlements follow the customer.

query: What routine tasks can customers complete themselves in the app instead of calling or visiting a branch?
gold_answer_summary: Through Meridian Pulse and Horizon Web customers can update personal details, order/manage and freeze cards with limits, set up and amend payments and standing instructions, apply for products, and raise/track service requests with in-app help. Because actions integrate with the core in real time they complete immediately, while sensitive actions require appropriate authentication.

query: If a customer misplaces their card, what can they sort out themselves in the app straight away?
gold_answer_summary: Customers can instantly freeze/unfreeze the card, report it lost or stolen and order a replacement, set or change the PIN, and apply spending controls such as per-channel limits or blocking online/overseas use. Changes integrate with card processing and the core, so they take effect in real time.

query: Can customers choose which account alerts they get and through which channel, and are any always sent?
gold_answer_summary: Yes — via ChannelOne's notification service customers opt into alerts (low balance, large/unusual transactions, payment receipts, card usage, bill reminders, security events) and choose channels (push, in-app, email, text), tuning them to avoid fatigue. Security-critical notifications such as a password change are always sent, and delivery is driven by real-time core/payments events.

query: How does the mobile app verify it's really the customer at login, and separately confirm a specific high-value payment?
gold_answer_summary: Login (kb_059): Meridian Pulse binds to the device at enrolment and uses the device's fingerprint/face biometric, verified locally so biometric data never reaches the bank, to unlock a stored credential, with a passcode fallback and configurable session/re-auth rules. Transaction signing (kb_060): for sensitive actions it re-prompts (e.g. biometric) and cryptographically signs the specific transaction details on the bound device, dynamically linking the approval to that exact amount and payee.

query: Do the mobile and web apps talk to the core directly, and how is duplicate-free, consistent behaviour ensured?
gold_answer_summary: No — Meridian Pulse and Horizon Web consume the ChannelOne services layer, which exposes APIs over core, payments, cards, and product services so business logic lives once in the services and both channels behave identically. ChannelOne calls underlying systems in real time, uses idempotency keys on writes to prevent duplicate payments from retries, applies consistent error handling and graceful degradation, and caches only where staleness is acceptable.

query: How is the mobile app hardened against cloning, interception, and running on a compromised device?
gold_answer_summary: Meridian Pulse establishes a cryptographic device binding at enrolment with keys in hardware-backed secure storage (so credentials can't be easily extracted or cloned), uses transport encryption with certificate pinning, and applies runtime protections detecting tampering/compromised devices while avoiding clear-text sensitive data. Auth combines device binding, biometrics, and dynamic transaction signing; sessions are time-limited, keys rotated, and security events feed Sentinel.

query: Can Meridian translate customer statements and the mobile app into local languages for multilingual customers?
gold_answer_summary: No supporting entry — multilingual translation/localisation of statements and the app is not covered in the KB (and is an explicitly out-of-scope area). Expected behaviour is to state no relevant content was found rather than fabricate a capability.

query: How does RegCast produce regulatory returns straight from operational data rather than from hand-compiled spreadsheets?
gold_answer_summary: RegCast draws data from across the platform (core, payments, treasury, trade, compliance) to produce regulatory returns reflecting actual positions/activity, preserving lineage back to source for audit. Reports can be scheduled, validated against rules before submission, and retained with versioning; report definitions are configuration, so changing requirements don't need source-system re-engineering.

query: At onboarding, how does GuardRail KYC verify a new customer's identity and decide how much due diligence they need?
gold_answer_summary: GuardRail KYC collects required identity/background data through guided journeys, verifies identity via document and data checks (and biometric/electronic verification where appropriate), and screens against sanctions/PEP lists via Sentinel. It captures beneficial ownership for entities, assigns an initial risk rating from the bank's model, and sets standard vs enhanced due diligence — straight-through for low-risk, routed for review otherwise, with evidence recorded for audit.

query: How does Sentinel cover both stopping payments to sanctioned parties and spotting suspicious behaviour by ordinary customers?
gold_answer_summary: Two complementary functions: real-time sanctions/PEP/watchlist screening with fuzzy matching holds payments to prohibited parties and re-screens customers on list updates (kb_072); behavioural transaction monitoring applies configurable scenarios — structuring, rapid in/out flows, profile-inconsistent activity, high-risk-jurisdiction flows — combined with the KYC risk rating to detect money laundering by otherwise-permitted customers (kb_073).

query: How does screening cope with spelling and name-order variations without drowning analysts in false positives?
gold_answer_summary: Sentinel normalises names (case, punctuation, transliteration) and applies algorithms tolerating spelling differences, reordering, missing/extra tokens, and phonetic similarity, scoring each candidate against tunable per-list/per-unit thresholds. Confirmed non-matches can be whitelisted under controls so benign parties aren't re-flagged, secondary data like date of birth discounts weak matches, and scores/decisions are retained for audit and tuning.

query: If a regulator questions a figure in a return, how can the bank trace it back to source and reproduce an old filing?
gold_answer_summary: RegCast extracts data per report specifications and transforms/aggregates it while preserving lineage — each figure traces back through the transformations to its source records. Reports are validated against structural/business rules before submission, each submission is versioned and retained with data as at the reporting point so historical returns reproduce exactly, and reconciliation checks confirm totals agree with source ledgers.

query: For a corporate owned through several layers of holding companies, how does KYC work out the real ultimate beneficial owners?
gold_answer_summary: GuardRail KYC models layered ownership across tiers and computes effective ownership percentages through the chain to identify individuals meeting the beneficial-ownership threshold, also recording control exercised by other means (e.g. senior managing officials where no qualifying owner exists) and flagging opaque/complex structures for enhanced scrutiny. Identified UBOs are screened via Sentinel, and the structure is revisited at review or on ownership change.

query: Does the platform manage physical branch operations like teller cash drawers, vault balancing, and ATM cash replenishment?
gold_answer_summary: No supporting entry — the KB covers digital channels and core processing but not branch cash handling, teller/vault management, or ATM cash logistics. Expected behaviour is to state no relevant content was found rather than fabricate a capability.
